title: prune-transcript staleness_check resolves root from raw payload cwd, diverging from the seam — CONFIRM-09 warnings silently never fire in cwd≠root sessions
evidence: `root = payload.get("cwd") or os.getcwd()` then `if not _hooklib.arbiter_active(root): return`. This is the one shared-core call site that bypasses the host seam's project_root entirely: no CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR (Claude), no git-toplevel resolution (either host). A session whose cwd is a subdirectory of the repo reads `<subdir>/.codearbiter/CONTEXT.md`, gets enabled=False, and the audit-staleness WARN (the CONFIRM-09 completeness control this branch explicitly registered on Codex too) silently never fires — and the whole degrade is inside `except Exception: pass`.
impact: WARN-only control lost without signal in exactly the sessions most likely to have drifted state (subdir/nested-cwd sessions); the two hosts and the other 15 entries all resolve root differently from this one, so behavior is inconsistent across hooks in the same session.
recommendation: Resolve root via _hooklib.project_root() (host seam), threading the payload once reliability-005's payload plumbing exists; keep the never-raise wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: staleness_check fires the CONFIRM-09 warn in an arbiter-enabled repo when the hook payload cwd is a subdirectory of the repo root
